questions.scenario: An App Service release used a staging slot swap. The new production path is bad, and the old slot is intact. What rollback shape fits first?

options.text: Swap back to the previous slot and verify user-path health.
Create a new App Service plan before changing traffic.
Delete the staging slot so the bad version cannot return.
Rotate every secret because rollback always requires secret rotation.

questions.explanation: For slot-based rollout, the natural rollback target is often the previous slot path, followed by verification.

questions.scenario: A Container Apps candidate revision at 20 percent traffic causes failures. The old revision is still healthy. Which rollback shape fits?

options.text: Route traffic back to the old revision and verify the failure rate returns to baseline.
Change the app name to force a new endpoint.
Raise traffic to 50 percent to confirm the pattern.
Rebuild the same image and hope for different runtime behavior.

questions.explanation: Revision traffic routing is the controlled rollback handle when the older revision is still a known healthy target.

questions.scenario: Which rollout details should be captured before release so rollback is not discovered during an incident?

options.text: Settings that are sticky or app-level.
The color used in the release calendar.

questions.explanation: Rollback needs a target, config scope, and recovery evidence.
